name: Castles of La Segarra – cycle touring in Catalonia
name_original: Castillos de la Segarra – Cicloturismo en Cataluña
description:
If you are passionate about the history of Catalonia, the route of this Route is ideal for you. The region of La Segarra is known as the land of castles, due to the large number of fortifications built there, some of which can be visited and are highly recommended. In the 11th-12th centuries in this place, crossed by the Sió River valley, the border between the Christian Catalan counties and Arab Al-Andalus was drawn. This turned this area into an unsafe space with strong defensive needs.

The tour begins in the town of Cervera, where you can arrive by train. To fully enjoy the experience and due to the unevenness, it is preferable to have a medium level of physical fitness. Although you can always use an electric bike or take a bus back in one of the towns it passes through.

A town on top of a hill presided over by a castle is a typical image of La Segarra, rightly called land of human towers. Along this Route you will get to know several of these specimens.

Cervera is the starting point of the Route. Here you can see the remains of his castle, which was a quadrangular fortress with cylindrical towers. A historical gem that is worth a visit.

After passing through the castle of Florejacs, you arrive at Les Palargues. It is an impressive fortress that has undergone various reforms, but still maintains a large Gothic arch at the entrance.

The current castle of Aranyó is the result of a complete remodeling in the 16th century. The writer Manuel de Pedrolo was born there. Now it is a private residence and its interior cannot be visited, but its magnificent architecture can be appreciated from the outside.

The small town of Montcortès has a magnificent castle declared a Cultural Asset of National Interest. Rectangular in plan, it exhibits two twin towers, a large voussoir doorway, Renaissance-style windows and a gallery of lowered arches on the upper floor.

With the image of all these fortresses, which speak of history and defy the passage of time, you arrive at Cervera, the capital of La Segarra and the end point of this tour.

The Route coincides in some sections with the route of the “Castells del Sió” Route (https://www.aralleida.cat/es/rutes/la-ruta-de-los-castillos-del-sio/).
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cervera traverse a region characterized by irregular and rugged terrain, encompassing mountains, forests, and valleys. Cyclists encounter diverse natural and cultivated landscapes, including dry stone features, ancient olive groves, and terraced land. The area features river valleys and high mountain meadows, offering varied elevations and scenic views. Best touring cycling routes around Cervera The most popular touring cycling route is Castles of La Segarra – cycle touring in Catalonia, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the historic castles of the La Segarra region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is InterCatalunya Cycle Route - Stage 2, a moderate 18.8 miles (30.2 km) path. This segment offers varied terrain as part of a larger regional route. Local touring cyclists also love the Camino Real through Fonolleres – Historic Center of Tàrrega loop from Cervera, a 16.3 miles (26.3 km) trail leading through historic areas, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Cervera is defined by river valleys, cultivated landscapes, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cervera?
A: Cervera offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 28 moderate and 29 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Cervera?
A: The region around Cervera is characterized by diverse and rugged terrain. You'll encounter a mix of natural and cultivated landscapes, including mountains, forests, and river valleys. Specific features include dry stone landscapes, ancient olive groves, terraced land, and high mountain meadows, providing varied elevations and scenic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Cervera?
A: While many routes in Cervera are classified as moderate to difficult, the region's diverse terrain means there are options suitable for varying fitness levels. For a moderate experience, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970570211/intercatalunya-cycle-route-stage-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;InterCatalunya Cycle Route - Stage 2&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain over 30.2 km. Always check the route details for elevation and surface conditions to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cervera?
A: The months of April and May are highly recommended for touring cycling in Cervera. During this period, the fields are vibrant with spring colors, and the weather is generally pleasant, making for an enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Cervera?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, the mix of natural and cultural attractions, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Cervera?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes that start and end in Cervera. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Real through Fonolleres – Historic Center of Tàrrega loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 26.3 km trail that takes you through historic areas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6955479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés – Castell de l'Aranyó loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, covering 43.6 km with moderate difficulty.

Q: What historical sites or cultural attractions can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cervera's touring cycling routes often pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can explore the historic castles of the La Segarra region on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1285583447/castles-of-la-segarra-cycle-touring-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles of La Segarra – cycle touring in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Murallat&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera&lt;/a&gt; itself has a charming old town worth visiting.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Cervera offers 29 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17549803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Cervera – Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 59.1 km and includes significant elevation changes, offering stunning views and demanding ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Cervera feature mixed terrain. While many segments are paved, you can also expect unpaved sections, especially in more rural or mountainous areas. This variety provides a diverse riding experience, from smooth roads to more rugged paths through forests and valleys.

Q: Are there any castles or fortified villages to visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical fortifications. You can visit several castles and fortified villages. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Murallat&lt;/a&gt;, a well-preserved medieval village, and various castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concabella Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes are designed to pass by these historic landmarks.

Q: Can I find places to eat or rest along the touring cycling paths?
A: Cervera itself is described as a beautiful town with an old town that offers services and restaurants. Many routes pass through or near charming towns and villages where you can find cafes and places to rest and refuel. It's always a good idea to check your chosen route for specific amenities along the way.
tips:
Meià Castle is part of the La Segarra Castle Route.
It is in good condition, but is currently closed.
Near it, there is a small temple of Romanesque origin. This is the church of the Mare de Déu de la Llet.
In the urban center of Guissona, is this museum of the ancient city of Iesso. It is a very interesting stop to learn how they lived in the ancient Roman population of the 1st century BC.

Sitges Castle is part of the La Segarra Castle Route.
It is located in the middle of cultivated fields, near the Florejacs castle. This building was also part of the border line between the Arab and Christian worlds during the time of the Reconquista. Can visit.

The Castle of les Pallargues, is a place that transports you to the medieval past. Highly recommended, the guided tour to get an idea of what it was like and how people lived there throughout its thousand years of history.

Castle that is part of the Segarra castles route.
Restored and with a great history. We will be able to go back in time with the information panels.
It is the birthplace of the writer Manuel de Pedrolo.
Apart from the castle, I recommend you to take a walk through the beautiful village with a medieval air among the cereal fields. Visit the Aranyó portal

waypoints.name: Guissona Museum – Ancient City of Iesso
